到.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 至 ('to arrive') + phonetic 刀.

以.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a person carrying something; the person is partly visible on the right in the modern form.

说.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 言 (speech) + phonetic 兌.

时.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 日 ('sun; day') + phonetic 寺.

要.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a person (later drawn as a woman 女) with two hands pointing to the midsection; originally meant 'waist'.

就.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound: originally 享 + 京 (temple and tower, 'to sacrifice at a high place'); 尤 was added later in seal script.

出.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's 齣 entry is a theatrical classifier character (phono-semantic: 齒 + 句), unrelated to the pictographic 出 ('to exit'); the trad field 齣 does not correspond to the formation described.
出.difference: The trad character 齣 is a distinct phono-semantic character used as a theatrical classifier, not the pictographic 出 (exit/emerge) our note describes; trad field appears to be an error.

会.wiki_summary: Pictogram: two open mouths with a food container in the middle, representing gathering/closing.
会.difference: Type mismatch: our data classifies 會 as compound_ideograph (会意), but Wiktionary classifies it as a pictogram (象形).

可.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound: semantic 口 + phonetic 丂, with reference to exhaled air; original meaning possibly 'to sing'.
可.difference: Type mismatch: our data classifies 可 as compound_ideograph (会意), but Wiktionary classifies it as a phono-semantic compound (形聲).

也.wiki_summary: Pictogram of a child (子) with mouth (口) open crying; arms were removed in bronze inscriptions and components fused in the modern form.
也.difference: Type mismatch: our data classifies 也 as simple_indicative (指事), but Wiktionary classifies it as a pictogram (象形).
